mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters in an enterprise environment. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to provide a robust and integrated experience. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with VMware tools and potential complexity in setup compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is necessary to manage Tanzu Kubernetes Grid.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

```

> Install the Tanzu CLI binary

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary configurations.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initializes the Tanzu CLI environment

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with specified version and node count.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

```

> Creates a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Fetches the kubeconfig for the cluster

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Lists all nodes in the cluster

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> This will delete the cluster and all associated resources

uninstall.title: Remove Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Removes the Tanzu CLI binary

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade the cluster version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup the cluster before upgrading
> Upgrades the cluster to the specified version
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: timeout waiting for cluster to be ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and ensure the network is properly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl && chmod +x ./kubectl && sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be correctly configured or the cluster certificate is invalid.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig using 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may have failed to start due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status with 'tanzu cluster list' and ensure all nodes are healthy.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.29, improved integration with Carvel tools.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to backup your clusters and check compatibility with existing workloads.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
